content.summary: Layer Health is a Cambridge, MA-based healthcare AI company (founded 2023) spun out of MIT, building an AI-powered Clinical Intelligence Platform that uses large language models (LLMs) to automate clinical data abstraction from medical records. The company is Series A-funded ($21M, March 2025; $25M total), backed by Define Ventures (lead), GV/Google Ventures, Flare Capital Partners, General Catalyst, MultiCare Capital Partners, and Inception Health. With ~55 employees, Layer Health targets health systems and life sciences organizations with a no-code/low-code SaaS platform that integrates directly with EHRs to support clinical registry reporting, revenue cycle management, quality measurement, and real-world evidence curation — without requiring labeled training data. Early deployments show 50–65%+ reductions in manual abstraction time. Key decision-makers include CEO David Sontag (MIT professor), CBO Alastair Blake (MD), CPO Brian Bhuta (ex-Press Ganey, Signify Health), and co-founders Divya Gopinath and Luke Murray (Co-Heads of Engineering). Strong buying signals include fresh capital deployment, active hiring, a growing reference customer roster, and imminent product expansion into clinical decision support and CDI. Data freshness: July 2026 (LinkedIn headcount, company page); March 2025 (funding round); July 2026 (company website team page).
content.industry: Healthcare AI / Software Development — AI-powered clinical data abstraction and chart review platform for health systems and life sciences
tech_stack: Large Language Models (LLMs) trained on longitudinal patient data — core AI engine
Clinical NLP / natural language processing for unstructured medical record extraction
No-code / low-code SaaS platform for self-service and enterprise deployment
Direct EHR integration (Epic and others) via HL7/FHIR
Enterprise-grade security and compliance (HIPAA-compliant; SOC-certified implied)
Machine learning for structured + unstructured clinical data (chart abstraction without labeled data)
content.headquarters: Cambridge, Massachusetts, USA (MIT spinout; team based in Greater Boston area)
key_investors: Define Ventures (led Series A; Lynne Chou O'Keefe, Managing Partner)
GV / Google Ventures (seed lead + Series A participation; Anthony Philippakis, Partner)
Flare Capital Partners (Series A; Parth Desai, Partner)
MultiCare Capital Partners (Series A strategic investor)
content.total_funding: ~$25 million ($4M seed in 2023 + $21M Series A in March 2025)
buying_signals: Recently closed $21M Series A (March 2025) — actively deploying capital for platform scaling and workforce expansion; company stated plans to 'scale its AI platform and expand its workforce'
Actively hiring — headcount grown to ~55 as of July 2026; LinkedIn shows job postings across ML engineer, clinical data analyst, customer success, and VP-level roles

New enterprise customer wins: White Plains Hospital case study published (50%+ abstractor time reduction); Froedtert & Medical College of Wisconsin deployment (65%+ time reduction); multi-year American Cancer Society deal signed early 2025
Market expansion into life sciences: self-service SaaS platform already live with life sciences customers; ACS partnership signals pharma/research vertical push beyond health systems

Product roadmap expansion: moving from clinical registry module to real-time clinical decision support, CDI (clinical documentation improvement), and medical coding modules — multiple new use cases opening up
Strategic institutional investor backing: GV (Google Ventures) involvement signals potential Google Cloud / healthcare AI ecosystem alignment

High conference and ecosystem visibility in 2025–2026: BOSTechWeek panelist, Flare Capital 'Healthcare AI Edition' featured speaker, Massachusetts AI Coalition events, CSAIL Entrepreneurship Day — indicating active BD and pipeline building
Engaged with major health system networks (Moffitt Cancer Center, White Plains, Froedtert) — suggesting an expanding reference customer base for outreach

Technology migration signal: the platform integrates with EHRs and replaces manual chart abstraction workflows — a strong displacement opportunity for health system vendors and workflow automation tools
content.headcount_estimate: ~55 employees (LinkedIn, July 2026; LinkedIn size bracket: 11–50, but live count shows 55)
